See how Rust's ownership model enables 300% throughput gains while eliminating entire classes of bugs. Perfect for systems programmers ready to build tomorrow's infrastructure safely and fast.

Title: Platform Engineering for Data Science at Scale: How Agile Infrastructure Unlocks 68% Faster ML Pipeline Delivery
Abstract: 58% of ML platforms fail to deliver value. Discover how platform engineering unlocks 68% faster model delivery and 37% higher AI success rates through cloud-native architecture and automated pipelines.

Abstract: We'll explore tangible strategies and direct applications to reduce developer cognitive load, shift from reactive to predictive operations, and boost overall efficiency. Discover how AI into platform tooling, with actionable examples you can apply immediately to deliver an exceptional DevX.

Abstract: From RAG to intelligent Q&A, semantic search, and log analytics — see how OceanBase brings native vector search and AI capabilities to Kubernetes platforms, combining multi-tenancy, strong consistency, and full SQL access in a single, production-ready database.

Title: Low-Code Meets Public Code: Automating with Minimal Engineering
Abstract: Explore how low-code platforms can augment public sector systems—saving time, reducing costs, and delivering faster.

Title: Fail Fast, Recover Faster: Harnessing Cascading Timeouts for Scalable System Resilience
Abstract: Struggling with slow failures and system crashes at scale? Learn how cascading timeouts transformed our infrastructure into a high-speed, failure-resilient machine. Walk away with proven tactics to boost throughput, cut latency, and build bulletproof MLOps systems.

Title: MLOps at Scale: Designing Governed AI Pipelines for Healthcare Impact
Abstract: Discover how to design secure, governed MLOps pipelines that scale across enterprise healthcare systems. Learn lessons from real-world deployments using GenAI, data governance frameworks, and AI-enhanced care workflows to accelerate innovation responsibly.

Title: Embracing Sky Computing: Charting a New Course in AI Cloud Infrastructure
Abstract: Unlock the future of AI product development with Sky Computing. Learn how seamless, intelligent cloud orchestration can revolutionize scalability, cost management, and compliance, paving the way for smarter, more resilient AI in production.

Title: From PoC to Production: Shipping Enterprise AI Agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
Abstract: Many AI agent projects stall at proof-of-concept because production demands security, scalability, and integration. Amazon Bedrock AgentCore changes this by providing managed services for identity, gateway-based tool access, runtime isolation, memory, and observability. In this talk, you’ll learn how to operationalize agents beyond chatbots: securely connecting to enterprise systems, handling long-running tasks, and migrating PoCs into production-ready deployments. We’ll explore patterns for authentication, memory, and monitoring—plus a blueprint you can apply to ship your next enterprise AI agent with confidence.

Title: Reimagining Incident Response with AI-Powered Visual Interfaces: From Static Dashboards to Real-Time Intelligence
Abstract: Discover how AI is revolutionizing incident response dashboards, cutting MTTR by 35%, automating visual insights, and enabling real-time decision-making. Learn from a $650B case study where smart visualization led to $42M in annual impact.

Title: AI-Driven Rate Limiting for Resilient and Cost-Efficient Cloud API Protection
Abstract: Discover how AI-powered rate limiting transforms API security! Learn to dynamically detect malicious traffic, cut false positives by 68%, and reduce infrastructure costs by 27%. Gain a proven, cloud-ready roadmap for resilient, scalable, and cost-efficient API protection.
Slides: Rehana Sultana Khan - Conf42 Incident Management 2025.pdf

Title: Resilient Cross-Cloud Analytics Pipelines with Azure Databricks: A 10TB+ Scale Reliability Engineering Case Study
Abstract: Discover how we engineered a resilient 10TB+/day analytics pipeline across Azure and AWS with 99.95% uptime, robust failure handling, and incident-aware orchestration ideal for reliability engineers managing critical data platforms at scale.
Slides: Sruthi Erra Hareram - Conf42 Incident Management 2025.pdf

Title: AI, Edge & Serverless: Reshaping Insurance Incident Response in the Cloud Era
Abstract: Discover how AI, edge, and serverless technologies are revolutionizing incident response in insurance slashing downtime, boosting agility, and transforming claims handling. Learn strategies to modernize, automate, and secure cloud-first operations at scale.

Title: When Compliance Meets Crisis: Managing Regulatory Incidents in HIPAA, GDPR, and DMA Environments
Abstract: System down. Regulators calling. Patients waiting. When compliance meets crisis, normal incident response fails spectacularly. Learn the battle-tested playbooks that prevented regulatory disasters at healthcare, fintech, and big tech companies.

JobTitle1: Assistant Vice President Systems, Investor Services Technology
Title: Securing AI-Driven Finance: Navigating Risks in Cloud-Native Modernization
Abstract: Discover how AI is transforming finance and why cloud-native adoption brings hidden risks. Learn real-world attack vectors, cutting-edge defenses like XAI and anomaly detection, and a practical roadmap to secure AI-driven systems without slowing innovation.

Title: Instant KAI Sandboxes with vCluster: Multi-Tenant, Multi-Scheduler GPU Sharing
Abstract: Sick of GPU deadlocks and cluster sprawl? In 30 min we’ll spin up vCluster sandboxes, drop NVIDIA’s open-source KAI Scheduler inside, and watch fractional GPUs push utilization from 40 % → 90 %. Multi-tenant, multi-scheduler, zero downtime—copy the YAML and go.

Abstract: Building distributed microservices is tough. Dapr, an open‑source app abstraction layer, streamlines microservice development by exposing features like state management, pub/sub, and more via standard APIs. Tech‑agnostic and Kubernetes‑friendly, it simplifies orchestration and delivery.

Title: Mirror Mirror on the Wall, How Many Kubernetes Environments Are Enough After All?

This talk will show how you can leverage existing envs for dev and CI so you can lower your cloud costs and reduce cluster sprawl.

Title: Observability in Kubernetes: Unlocking the Power of eBPF
Abstract: Discover how eBPF-powered observability can facilitate proactive incident detection, root cause analysis, and rapid remediation, enabling you to stay ahead of potential issues in your Kubernetes ecosystem.

Title: Level Up: Why TypeScript/JavaScript for game development?
Abstract: Discover how TypeScript and JavaScript empower web developers to create modern games, and why now is the best time! Learn about tools, workflows, and real-world examples that make these languages ideal for building cross-platform interactive experiences on the web and mobile apps!

JobTitle1: Senior API and DevX Content Writer
Title: Making Docs and AI Work Together: Improving Context, Accuracy, and Agentic Coding in the IDE
Abstract: AI can speed up the workflow, but without the right context, it can just as easily slow it down. This session explores how to balance AI assistance with documentation to maintain accuracy and control inside the IDE. Learn how modern APIs like Cloudinary, Auth0, and Firebase can work hand in hand with AI tools to generate cleaner code, automate tasks safely, and accelerate development without guesswork.

Title: Accessible JavaScript: it's easier than you think!
Abstract: Twenty percent of the population has a disability: that means your users! It's easy enough to make your forms and JavaScript accessible, if you know how. Gian Wild gives concrete code examples and explains the three types of JavaScript functionality and the ten JavaScript and form principles.

Title: The 2025 State of JavaScript Testing
Abstract: This talk will go over what changed it the past decade regarding testing Web applications. We will focus on practices, patterns, and library changes and learn what the future holds for us around test runners, testing libraries, mocking, production practices, and AI-based tools.

Title: Let Your Browser Take a Breather with a Scheduler.yield()
Abstract: Modern web applications often overload the browser’s main thread, causing sluggish interfaces and poor responsiveness. This talk explores how JavaScript’s single-threaded execution model contributes to that problem, and how the new `scheduler.yield()` API offers a clean, effective way to give control back to the browser without breaking your code flow. We’ll walk through the limitations of traditional approaches, introduce task prioritization with `scheduler.yield()`, and show practical examples of how to make apps feel faster and more responsive. By the end, you’ll know how to let your browser take a breather, and your users will feel the difference.

Title: From Prompts to Protection: How Security Copilot Reinvents Cyber Defense
Abstract: Cyber defense is no longer just written in code or policy, it’s spoken in prompts. I’ll explore how Security Copilot turns those prompts into protection by optimizing Conditional Access, enforcing Zero Trust, and transforming AI insights. Join me to see how words become your line of defense.

Title: AI as Your Agile Copilot: Predictable, Risk-Aware, and Human-Centric Delivery in the Age of Prompt Engineering
Abstract: Learn how AI and prompt engineering can cut Agile delivery overruns, improve estimation accuracy, and detect risks before they derail projects while keeping your teams human-centric, ethical, and high-performing.

Title: Designing Data Science Careers Through A/B Testing and Experimentation Mastery
Abstract: Master the art of A/B testing to supercharge your data science career! Learn how top-tier experimentation, statistical precision, and real-world impact can set you apart in AI-driven roles—from optimizing products to fine-tuning LLMs. Don’t just analyze—experiment!
Slides: Bhavana Reddy Chadagonda - Conf42 Prompt Engineering 2025.pdf

Company1: Manager Data Management At Ally Bank

Abstract: Discover how Data Lakehouse Architecture unifies the power of data lakes and warehouses to deliver real-time, AI-ready insights at scale. Learn best practices to cut costs, streamline workflows, and fuel next-gen prompt engineering innovation.

Title: Strategic Role of AI-Powered Recommendation Systems in Shaping Industries
Abstract: Discover how AI-powered recommendation systems are transforming from simple suggestion engines into strategic infrastructure driving personalization, efficiency, and competitive advantage across industries from e-commerce to robotics-enabled marketplaces.

Title: Securing Robot Networks for the Quantum Era
Abstract: Quantum computers could soon compromise robotic systems. Learn how to secure your robot networks with post-quantum cryptography, using NIST standards and real-world case studies without breaking real-time performance.

Abstract: Discover how a Unified AI Data Governance Framework can supercharge robotics in banking cutting AI deployment time by 30%, ensuring global compliance, and enabling real-time oversight across massive, fast-moving data ecosystems.

Title: AI-Driven Emergency Response: Cloud, Robotics, and Predictive Analytics
Abstract: Discover how AI, robotics, and cloud-powered predictive analytics are transforming disaster response. From real-time data to autonomous systems, learn how smarter emergency frameworks can save lives and build resilient cities.

Title: How AI can enhance digital forensic analysis and evidence collection
Abstract: In a world where cybercriminals evolve faster, AI is revolutionizing digital forensics, uncovering evidence, helping investigations, and automating analysis. Discover how AI-driven forensics enhances accuracy, speeds up case resolution, and reshapes future cybersecurity investigations.

Title: Accelerating Enterprise Migrations with Secure and Scalable Automation

Title: Supercharging DevOps with MCP (Without Opening a Security Hole)

JobTitle1: Business Program Manager - Lead Technical Trainer
Title: A lap around GitHub Dependabot, CodeQL and other built-in security features
Abstract: Like any other development process, security should be at the forefront of the dev cycle. That's where GitHub provides built-in solutions with Dependabot, CodeQL and several others. Learn in this session how to enable security features to protect code from vulnerabilities, full demos, no slides...

Title: Understanding SAST Foundations with Open-Source Semgrep
Abstract:
Static Application Security Testing (SAST) is a fundamental practice for securing the software development lifecycle, but its inner workings are often treated as a complex black box. This session peels back those layers, focusing on the core principles of static analysis using the fast, open-source Semgrep engine as our practical guide. We will explore how modern SAST moves beyond simple text searching by parsing code into Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs) to understand its true structure and intent.

On that foundation, we will start with Semgrep (semgrep.dev), a fast, open-source SAST engine that matches code shapes across many languages. You’ll learn Semgrep’s minimal building blocks: YAML rules, patterns, and pattern combinators, metavariables and constraints, severity and metadata, and autofix for developer-friendly guidance. We’ll also cover when to switch from simple structural matching to taint mode (sources, sinks, sanitizers) for data-flow findings. Finally, we outline the basics of adopting Semgrep, including running it locally and in CI, as well as starting with community rule packs and testing rules.

Attendees leave with a clear mental model of how SAST works and a practical, open-source starting point to add precise, maintainable code checks to their DevSecOps workflows.

Title: STEM in the Garden: How to Monitor Plants with IoT Sensors and Grafana Cloud
Abstract:
Are you a parent who wants to do a fun project with your kids? Are you trying to keep your plants alive? Or are you considering using an IoT device to monitor your plant's soil moisture level?

If any of the following resonates, this talk is for you!
Slides: Marie Cruz - Conf42 Internet of Things (IoT) 2025.pdf

Title: IoT Supply Chain Security: Defending Connected Device Ecosystems from Cyberattacks
Abstract: Learn proven strategies to defend IoT ecosystems from supply chain cyberattacks. Discover how hardware attestation, network segmentation, and behavioral analytics can reduce IoT security incidents by 60%+ based on real-world data
Slides: Gresshma Atluri - Conf42 Internet of Things (IoT) 2025.pdf

Title: IoT Data Lakehouse: Scalable Architectures for Connected Device Analytics
Abstract: Learn how IoT Lakehouse architectures solve massive sensor data challenges by unifying streaming telemetry, device logs, and analytics in scalable platforms that power predictive maintenance, smart city monitoring, and connected device intelligence.
Slides: Bhanudeepti Chinta - Conf42 Internet of Things (IoT) 2025.pdf

JobTitle1: Driving Strategic Growth for Global Customers
Title: Taming IoT Data Streams: Lessons in Managing Scale, Complexity, and Trust
Abstract: IoT devices generate massive streams of data, but the challenge is making that data reliable, secure, and usable at scale. Engineers face messy pipelines, outages, and governance needs. By improving how we manage streaming systems like Kafka, teamsfocus on building IoT apps that deliver real impact.

Title: Observing Agentic/MCP Traffic & Keeping Costs Low
Abstract: Agentic Workloads and MCP is at the forefront of every engineering organization in the cloud-native realm. That's why there are two major questions coming up. 1). How can we observe metrics. 2) How can we control posts. In this session, you'll learn how to produce Agentic and MCP traffic

Title: Context Engineering for DevOps: Turning Your Infrastructure into a Reasoning Graph
Abstract: LLMs fail at infrastructure because they can't capture live dependencies or multi-hop relationships. This talk shows how to treat infrastructure as a graph so AI systems can reason instead of guess. Learn Graph RAG, natural language to Cypher querying, and how to measure context quality.

Title: Defender XDR in the Real World: From Alert Fatigue to Measurable Risk Reduction
Abstract: Real-world Defender XDR playbook that turns alert storms into actionable incidents, applies safe automations to triage and contain, and cuts MTTR. Clear guidance on tuning built-ins vs custom detections and a simple metric pack to prove risk reduction.

Title: How AI is redefining SRE and Customer Experience
Abstract: AI is reshaping SRE, but the real opportunity isn’t faster RCA, it’s building proactive, customer-centered reliability. This talk explores how AI and culture together move us from firefighting to foresight.

Title: From Zero to a DevSecOps Hero: Extending Microsoft Sentinel
Abstract: Become a DevSecOps hero with Microsoft Sentinel. Learn how to impress your SecOps colleagues by building custom connectors, analytics rules, and playbooks; enhancing detection and remediation capabilities, and shortening the time required to detect and mitigate attacks.

Title: Minimum Viable AIMS: ISO 42001 Required Documentation Artifacts for Beginners
Abstract: Experimenting with LLMs is fun until someone asks: who approved this, mwhat changed, what data was used, and can we explain it? Let's demystify "Minimum Viable AIMS": ISO 42001 documentation artifacts that make your organization’s AI management system audit-ready.

Title: Building Secure Backend Services with AI Agents
Abstract: As AI agents become part of everyday engineering, building secure backend systems is more critical than ever. Marat Kenzhebulatov will show how to integrate AI agents safely in security-critical workflows, from code review and threat modeling to automated checks, without exposing sensitive data. Attendees will leave with practical guidelines, lightweight patterns, and a checklist for leveraging AI agents while maintaining real-world security and compliance.

Title: Beyond Send-Time Optimization: From Prediction to AI-Driven Decisioning in B2C Marketing
Abstract:
Send-time optimization has traditionally been treated as a prediction problem—using historical data to determine when customers are most likely to engage. While effective in simpler environments, this approach falls short in today’s B2C landscape, where customers receive multiple messages across channels, campaigns compete for attention, and engagement is constrained by fatigue, regulations, and business priorities. In this context, selecting the “best” send time is no longer just a modeling task, but a decision-making challenge.

This session reframes send-time optimization as an AI-driven decision system that combines machine learning and artificial intelligence in complementary ways. Machine learning provides predictive signals such as engagement likelihood and time-based patterns, while AI adds reasoning, orchestration, and goal awareness—resolving tradeoffs between urgency, relevance, frequency, and customer experience in real time.
Slides: Murthy Chandrapaty & Chakravarthy Kalva - Conf42 Machine Learning 2026.pdf

Title: Why Good Models Fail After Deployment
Abstract: Great models don’t usually fail because they’re wrong - they fail because the world changes after they’re deployed. This talk explains why strong offline performance is often a poor predictor of real-world success, and how feedback loops, biased data, and misaligned metrics quietly break machine learning systems in production. Learn how to build systems that survive the real world!

Title: AI-Driven Engineering Quality: How We Use Machine Learning to Predict Delivery Time and Improve Code Reliability
Abstract: Shipping breaks when planning is guesswork. I’ll show a production ML system that scores pull requests for risk, estimates delivery time before coding starts, and flags bottlenecks, using repo metadata, AST features, test/CI signals, and LLM-based code smells, fully integrated into CI.

Title: AI-Driven Risk-Aware Decision-Making for Scalable Reliability Systems
Abstract: Resilience in Site Reliability Engineering encompasses more than just failure detection; it also includes the ability to make informed choices amidst uncertainty. This talk delves into how AI-driven, risk-aware decision-making extends automation, optimizes risk management, and guarantees the construction of scalable, high-reliability systems.
Slides: Oreoluwa Omoike - Conf42 Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 2026.pdf

Title: Why Kubernetes Default Load Balancing Doesn’t Work for HTTP/2 Traffic
Abstract:
When we introduced HTTP/2 traffic into our Kubernetes environment, we observed a puzzling incident: one backend pod consistently showed high CPU usage and latency, while others remained almost completely idle. Node and cluster metrics indicated sufficient capacity, yet performance degradation persisted.

The root cause was a misunderstanding of how Kubernetes Services and kube-proxy handle traffic. By default, Kubernetes performs Layer-4 (TCP) load balancing at the connection level. While this works well for HTTP/1.x with short-lived connections, HTTP/2 multiplexing allows many requests over a single connection — causing all traffic to be routed to one pod.

In this talk, I’ll break down what happened, why traditional assumptions about load balancing failed, and how introducing Layer-7 routing through a service mesh resolved the issue. Attendees will gain practical insights into HTTP/2 behavior, kube-proxy limitations, and real-world traffic engineering lessons.
Slides: Mariem Sboui - Conf42 Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 2026.pdf

Title: Beyond Observability: Proactive SRE for AI-Driven Personalisation
Abstract:
Financial pricing systems are SRE's ultimate test: sub-50ms decisions, regulatory audits, market volatility, zero-downtime model updates. At Lionsoul Global (WealthTech), I've engineered pricing engines for HNWI portfolios that survive black swan events - blending telco-scale patterns with fintech constraints.

AI personalisation engines (recommendations, dynamic pricing, client journeys) fail silently - model drift kills LTV silently too. At Lionsoul Global (WealthTech), I run SRE for AI pipelines powering HNWI portfolios, proactively catching issues before revenue impact.

Key patterns:

- Proactive signals: Custom metrics beyond golden signals — propensity drift, uplift decay, A/B divergence alerts (Grafana + ClickHouse)

- Small-data SRE: Bayesian priors + relationship manager feedback loops when training data = dozens of clients (not millions)

- Experiment resilience: Chaos engineering for model swaps, canary deploys preserving personalization continuity

- Compliance automation: Audit trails for AI decisions, human-in-loop guardrails via LLM copilots

Real impact: 25% LTV uplift sustained across model versions; 15% incremental revenue from telco-scale patterns adapted to fintech. Code frameworks + alerting configs included.

Walk away with SRE patterns that keep AI personalisation profitable when the models inevitably start decaying.
Slides: Yury Lysak - Conf42 Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 2026.pdf

Title: Scale or Fail as Spotify's Growth Exposed the Abstraction Paradox
Abstract: At Spotify, serving 675M users, hyper-growth in 2014 created system fragmentation that made onboarding engineers difficult. Our abstractions oversimplified complexity, hindering incident response. We learned effective abstraction teaches complexity rather than hiding it.
Slides: Stuart Clark - Conf42 Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 2026.pdf

Title: From Vibes to Outages: When AI Writes the Code You Debug
Abstract:
AI-assisted coding is exploding. But this acceleration doesn't translate into reliability. Quite the opposite.

This talk dives into the quirks of LLM-assisted development with real-world examples: hard-to-trace bugs, AI-generated tests that mirror flawed logic, and hallucinated dependencies that open security gaps. We'll examine the operational fallout: skyrocketing code churn, higher incident rates, and large batch deployments that make debugging harder.

As developers become less familiar with their own code, leaner SRE teams deal with the consequences. We'll explore actionable strategies, from AI-powered incident tools to "incident vibing."
Slides: Sylvain Kalache - Conf42 Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 2026.pdf

Title: Reduce AI Agents Costs and Mistakes with Semantic tool Selection
Abstract: AI agents with many tools face a dual problem: they pick the wrong tool and waste tokens because tool descriptions get serialized into the context on every call. As agents scale to 50+ tools, errors increase and costs explode. Semantic tool selection filters tools before they reach the LLM context using vector search, reducing errors and token costs. This talk walks you through building TWO versions of the same travel agent to prove semantic tool selection works. By comparing the agents side-by-side on identical queries, you'll see dramatic improvements in both accuracy and cost and learn how to implement dynamic tool filtering that preserves conversation memory across multi-turn sessions. You'll leave with working Python code and production patterns you can deploy immediately.
Slides: Elizabeth Fuentes Leone - Conf42 Cloud Native 2026.pdf

Title: Design Systems for Scalable Cloud-Native Analytics Platforms
Abstract: Cloud-native analytics platforms must deliver consistency, accessibility, and performance across distributed teams, services, and interfaces. This session explores the architectural foundations required to build scalable design systems in modern cloud-native environments. It focuses on five measurable pillars: component modularity, token-driven theming, cross-platform consistency, versioning discipline, and governance maturity. We begin with component modularity, showing how decoupled UI primitives enable independent deployment, reduce duplication, and accelerate delivery across microservices-driven teams. The session then examines token-based theming, where design tokens act as a single source of truth for visual and interaction properties, enabling consistent updates across dynamically deployed applications. Next, we address cross-platform consistency, outlining patterns that ensure cohesive experiences across web dashboards, embedded analytics, mobile applications, and operational tools in distributed ecosystems. Attendees will learn how to maintain parity while supporting product-level flexibility. We also cover versioning strategies critical in cloud-native systems, including semantic versioning, release channels, and backward compatibility approaches that prevent breaking dependent services. Finally, we explore governance models that scale with organizational complexity, including contribution workflows, accessibility standards, and decision frameworks that balance autonomy with alignment.

Title: Keep Calm and Run on AWS: Engineering Compliance Without Abandoning Your Stack
Abstract:
Your datacenter vendor says you need on-premises infrastructure. The sovereignty crowd says you need seven EU-native providers. Your legal team forwards another GDPR article at 9am. Customers are refusing after reading biased articles in the newspaper paid by the local DC owner. Your engineers just want to ship.

This session is a practitioner's defence of running AWS in regulated European environments — what actually needs solving, what is compliance theater, and how to build a stack your engineering team can still operate at 3am. We'll cover what Outposts genuinely solves and where it quietly fails — control plane metadata, backup destinations, and the DR trap nobody puts in the sales deck. We'll look at real capacity planning and benchmarking: what the performance delta actually looks like and what causes it. And we'll make the case against trading a single well-governed AWS account for seven EU vendors with seven SLAs, seven blast radiuses, and seven on-call rotations. Finally — AWS European Sovereign Cloud launched in January 2026. We'll cut through the noise on CLOUD Act, DORA, and NIS2 to show where ESC moves the needle and where the legal gaps remain.

Sovereignty without operational maturity is just compliance theater. Let's talk about what real compliance engineering looks like.

Title: Stablecoins as Cloud-Native Payment Infrastructure
Abstract:
Cloud architects face a new reality: payment infrastructure is decentralizing. Stablecoins solve cross-border friction at internet speed, but require production-grade reliability - think CloudWatch for onchain reserves, Kubernetes for validator fleets, Terraform for compliance configs. Stablecoins represent the first cloud-native payment infrastructure - programmable, global, 24/7 settlement without intermediaries. This talk dissects their architecture through real-world deployments: Polygon's scaling to 1B+ tx, El Salvador's Chivo Wallet (national Bitcoin rollout).  I'll cover:

- Technical stack: Onchain observability, reserve monitoring, real-time compliance
- Custody & settlement: Multi-sig, institutional-grade rails vs traditional banking
- Scaling patterns: From testnet to production (Polygon's infra evolution)
- Cloud integration: API layers, event streaming, hybrid custody models

The talk bridges Web3 realities with cloud-native thinking: what breaks at scale, how to monitor programmable money, and why traditional payment processors can't compete on latency/cost. Attendees leave with concrete patterns for building/maintaining stablecoin infrastructure in hybrid cloud environments.

Title: Building a Production Computer Vision Verification System for Warehouse Receiving
Abstract: I will present a case study of designing and deploying a computer vision verification system for warehouse receiving in an industrial supply chain. The session focuses not only on detecting visually similar spare parts, but also on how the solution was embedded into an existing operational workflow under real production constraints. It covers the end-to-end design of an AI-assisted receiving process, including image-based verification, the use of catalog and technical reference data, and integration with warehouse operations. Special attention is given to confidence thresholds, exception handling, human-in-the-loop validation, service reliability, and the trade-offs between accuracy and throughput in a time-sensitive environment. Attendees will also see how the system was operated in practice, how user interaction was designed for warehouse personnel, and how the deployed solution improved receiving quality and operational consistency while reducing spare-parts mismatches by 99%.

Title: From Code Correctness to Behavior Validation: Detecting Defects Early in Embedded Automotive Software
Abstract: In embedded automotive software, implementation correctness is only part of the reliability challenge. Control logic may be correctly written, compiled, and unit-tested, yet still fail at the system level when real-world behavioral expectations, integration assumptions, and cross-module interactions are not fully validated early in development.  As chassis and powertrain platforms become increasingly software-defined and interconnected, late-stage defects are more often caused by behavioral mismatches than by coding errors alone. These issues typically surface during system integration or vehicle-level testing, where diagnosis and correction are far more costly.  This session introduces a behavior-driven validation approach that shifts validation earlier in the development lifecycle. By explicitly defining expected system behavior and continuously validating it throughout design and implementation, teams can identify defects sooner, improve traceability between intent and execution, and reduce the risk of late-stage surprises.  Drawing from safety-critical automotive software contexts, this talk will show how behavior-driven validation frameworks help engineering teams detect integration defects earlier, align actual system behavior with design intent, and build greater confidence in complex embedded software systems.

JobTitle1: Senior Software Developer and Team Lead
Title: Engineering High-Performance Real-Time Leaderboards: From O(N²) to O(log N)
Abstract: In this technical session, Ivan Balashov explores how to design and implement high-performance leaderboards capable of handling 100K+ concurrent users with real-time updates. He presents a case study where a production system suffered from daily outages due to O(N²) update complexity, CPU spikes, and memory pressure. The talk covers the end-to-end solution: an indexed skip list augmented with span counters for O(log N) position queries, combined with a hash-based user deduplication system. Ivan shares practical lessons from replacing a naive slice-based leaderboard with a memory-efficient, concurrency-safe, in-process solution delivered in just 2 days. Attendees will gain insight into probabilistic data structures, cache-aware design, and how to pragmatically balance technical excellence with urgent business requirements in production-critical systems.

Title: Modularizing a 10-Year Monolith: The Architecture, The People, and the Pain
Abstract: Most Go codebases begin as straightforward layered monoliths, but years of growth often turn those layers into a web of hidden coupling, unclear ownership, and hard-to-predict side effects. Small changes start requiring deep context, and compile and test times slowly creep up, turning routine work into risky work. Rewrites promise a clean slate but rarely succeed in practice. What the Go community lacks are real examples of large open source Go projects that have successfully evolved toward a modular monolith.  This talk presents a major open source Go project in the middle of that evolution. It covers how we are moving from a decade-old layered architecture toward a modular design while continuing to ship features to multiple production environments that teams actively depend on every day. This is not theory. This is architectural change in a live system, with real contributors, long CI pipelines, social constraints, and legacy assumptions embedded throughout the code.  What began as an investigation into slow build and test times exposed deeper problems: oversized packages, uncontrolled dependencies, unclear boundaries, and an architecture that no longer matched how engineers actually reasoned about the system. We walk through familiar pain points in large Go monoliths, including tight coupling, long feedback cycles, and frequent merge conflicts, and explain why these problems persist even in well-intentioned codebases. You’ll see where our early attempts stalled, how architectural changes regressed quietly over time, why good ideas alone were not enough, and how steady, incremental refactoring helped us regain control without freezing development. Architecture only works when people agree to carry it together.  Whether you are working on a fast-growing Go project or maintaining a mature production system, this talk will give you concrete techniques and mental models for evolving your architecture safely. You’ll leave with a clearer understanding of how to introduce boundaries that hold, align ownership with code, and make a large system feel smaller, safer, and easier to change, so teams can move faster without needing to hold the entire system in their heads.

Title: Building Secure CI/CD Pipelines with Go-Based Automation
Abstract: Modern platform engineering teams are increasingly using Go to build fast, reliable, and cloud-native automation tools for CI/CD and infrastructure management.  In this talk, I explore how Go can be used to design secure, scalable CI/CD automation components that support modern DevOps pipelines. Drawing from real-world architecture experience across AWS, Azure, and GCP, I demonstrate how Go-based tooling can improve deployment reliability, enforce governance policies, and reduce operational risk.  We will focus on practical engineering patterns rather than theory, including how Go services can be integrated into CI/CD workflows, how to design for observability and resilience, and how to structure automation systems that scale across multi-cloud environments.  Attendees will gain insights into:  Why Go is well-suited for platform engineering and CI/CD automation How to design secure pipeline automation services using Go Common pitfalls when building infrastructure tooling Architecture patterns for scalable DevOps systems  This is a practical, experience-driven session aimed at engineers building real-world delivery platforms.

Title: Digital PMOs in Retail with Jira and Confluence: Governance Through Transparency
Abstract: Retail technology portfolios that span e commerce, supply chain, and in store platforms often struggle under traditional PMO models. Status tracking is frequently fragmented across spreadsheets and disconnected tools, making it difficult to maintain a single source of truth, identify dependencies early, or apply consistent governance controls. These gaps slow decision making, reduce transparency, and create operational inefficiencies across the portfolio.  This session presents a Digital PMO framework that integrates Jira and Confluence to establish a unified governance and execution environment. The framework translates established PMO and IT governance concepts into a practical operating model built on structured workflows, standardized templates, and integrated documentation. It connects strategic themes, initiatives, OKRs, epics, delivery telemetry, risk signals, and executive reporting within a single system of record that supports both engineering teams and leadership.  A retail case study demonstrates the framework’s application within an Automotive Care Center technology portfolio that involved more than 20 cross functional teams and approximately 50 initiatives per quarter over a six month period. Quantitative results included a 30 percent reduction in PMO operational effort within six months and an additional 30 percent reduction at 12 months, while program manager roles were reduced from eight to three. Stakeholders also reported improved transparency, faster trade off decisions, and greater trust in portfolio reporting.  Attendees will learn how integrated Jira and Confluence environments can support scalable governance, real time portfolio visibility, and more efficient program management in complex retail technology ecosystems.

Title: RAG in production: challenges, techniques and solutions
Abstract: You may have seen reports of AI models suggesting eating rocks or putting glue on pizza. RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) is an architecture designed to prevent these kinds of hallucinations. RAG contains two elements: a “retriever” (vector database) to obtain contextual information, and a generator (LLM) to produce the final response. It sounds simple, but implementing and deploying RAG in production has its own challenges. In this talk, you’ll learn about some of these challenges, as well as techniques to mitigate or eliminate them.

Title: Doing more with Cloud API's
Abstract: All the major Cloud vendors provide customers with a rich api based capability to manage your environment. In many cases these apis provide more functionality than is available through the Web console, but less easy to use. By extracting api output and loading into a database this allows you to perform complex analysis on that data, and build applications to enhance your Cloud experience

Title: Schema Migrations in CI/CD: When AI Helps, When It Hurts, and How to Tell the Difference
Abstract: Schema migrations are the riskiest deploy most teams ship. Application releases can be rolled forward and back. A migration that locks a 2TB table for an hour, or quietly loses data on rollback, cannot. And now AI wants to write them for us. This talk is a working CI/CD field guide for shipping database changes safely in cloud environments, and an honest assessment of where AI-assisted review fits inside that pipeline. We'll cover the migration patterns that hold up: backward-compatible schema changes, expand-and-contract releases, automated migration tests, approval gates, and post-deploy verification. Then we'll add the AI layer where it earns its keep, including drafting forward and rollback DDL, flagging unsafe operations before review (full-table rewrites, locks held in transactions, missing concurrency hints), and triaging incidents when a migration goes sideways. I'll also be direct about where AI breaks down. Hallucinated database syntax, like Postgres flags that are actually MySQL flags. Missing the operational context that a "trivial" migration on a hot table will lock production for an hour. Rollback scripts that compile but quietly lose rows. The framework, three axes for scoring any migration workflow on blast radius, reversibility, and human-in-loop requirement, gives engineers a defensible way to decide where automation and AI belong in their pipeline, and where they don't. Attendees leave with concrete CI/CD patterns they can adopt next sprint, the specific failure modes to design around, and a practical decision rule for keeping engineering judgement in control of production data.

Title: Can a Kubernetes Operator Replace a DBA? A 30-Day Experiment
Abstract: Kubernetes operators promise to automate complex operational tasks, backups, failovers, upgrades, scaling, and turn database management into a declarative workflow. But how far does that promise actually go?  In this talk, I run a 30-day experiment to find out.  Using a PostgreSQL cluster deployed with the CloudNativePG operator, I handed over day-to-day database operations to Kubernetes and observed what happened in real-world conditions. Over the course of a month, I monitored the system while intentionally introducing common production problems: node failures, pod crashes, storage pressure, replication issues, upgrades, and backup recovery scenarios.  Some problems were resolved automatically within seconds. Others exposed surprising limitations where manual intervention was still required.  This talk walks through the experiment week by week, showing what the operator handled gracefully, what broke, and where human operators are still essential. Along the way we’ll explore the operational model behind Kubernetes operators, the trade-offs of automated database management, and the practical lessons teams should know before trusting automation with their stateful workloads.  If you’ve ever wondered whether operators can truly replace traditional database administration, or simply shift the role, this experiment provides a candid, data-driven answer.  Key Takeaways  Attendees will learn:  - How Kubernetes operators manage stateful systems like PostgreSQL - Which database failures operators can automatically recover from - Where manual operational knowledge is still required - The operational trade-offs of running databases in Kubernetes - Practical lessons from a month-long real-world experiment

Title: Five Hard Lessons from Five Years of So-Called Serverless Databases
Abstract: After five years of managing serverless databases, I have learned that my rollercoaster journey is very similar to the CPU usage you dream of seeing in the console. This session shares five hard-earned lessons learned while working with so-called serverless databases.

Title: Beyond the Context Window: Building a "Mental Model" for Your AI Agents
Abstract: Weve all seen what happens when you give an LLM a single repo: its usually quite successful. But what happens when you give it a distributed system with 200 microservices? It gets lost in the "muddy middle". The bottleneck in AI-driven development isn't code generation; its architectural context.  This talk introduces the Application Architecture Hub - a living, AI-maintained knowledge base designed specifically for agent consumption.   We will explore how to move away from static, stale documentation and toward a system of automated "facets" (APIs, Domain Models, Events, and more) that allow AI agents to troubleshoot, design, and assess impact across your entire ecosystem. We'll explore how this can benefit workflows beyond coding, across the organization.  Attendees will leave with a blueprint for building an ingestion pipeline that keeps their AI agents (and their humans) in sync with the "truth" of the codebase.
Slides: Lior Schejter - Conf42 Large Language Models (LLMs) 2026.pdf

Title: Automating Database Operations with Cortex Code
Abstract: With Cortex Code and the power of DBT, SQL, Python and autonomous agents we can easily manage and build advanced automation, continuous integration, continuous deployment and version control.   By using Snowflake authored skills, Cortex Code CLI allows for natural language to manage schema creation and change, with full testing and automated deployments.
Slides: Tim Spann - Conf42 Large Language Models (LLMs) 2026.pdf

Title: Building an ML Automoderation System for LLM-Augmented Online Platforms
Abstract: In this session, I'll explain how Social Discovery Group built a production ML automoderation system for user-to-user messaging at scale. The talk covers the full workflow: collecting/aligning chat data with Trust & Safety policies, hybrid models (rules + MLLMs for grey areas), thresholding under extreme imbalance, and routing to reviewer queues with priority scoring.  We'll dive into: - Data prep: sampling rare violations, policy-aligned labels. - Model stack: lightweight classifiers for high-risk (toxicity, spam), MLLMs for nuanced cases (harassment, grooming). - Evaluation: imbalance-aware metrics (precision@low recall), error analysis loops with moderator feedback. - Production: A/B thresholds, monitoring drift, safe rollouts as abuse evolves.  This system processes millions of messages daily, reducing reviewer load by 60% while catching 95%+ of violations. Attendees will get a blueprint for LLM-powered moderation pipelines that balance safety, scale, and false positives  critical for LLM-generated content and agentic interactions.
Slides: Andrei Shcherbinin - Conf42 Large Language Models (LLMs) 2026.pdf

Title: Clean and Controlled AI with API Management
Abstract: Everyone uses AI to improve their organization, but if you are not careful, youll drain your budget, increase energy usage, or create risks you never intended. Do you keep a leash on your AI?  In this session I will demonstrate how to bring solid governance and sustainability into your AI landscape using Azure API Management and Azure AI Foundry. You will learn about practical controls such as content filtering, token-aware throttling, and caching. I will show you when and how to implement these controls to create safer and more sustainable AI systems.  If you want your organization to waste less energy on AI systems while making them safer to use, this talk is for you!
Slides: Leo Visser - Conf42 Large Language Models (LLMs) 2026.pdf

Title: Building Reliable Infrastructure for LLM Workloads on Kubernetes
Abstract: I will explain what teams need to get right in Kubernetes before LLM services become reliable enough for real-world use, as running LLM workloads in production is not only a model problem but also an infrastructure one. The session will focus on the operational side of LLM platforms, including cluster design, networking, redundancy, secrets handling, and control-plane stability in environments where teams need predictable behavior under load. Attendees will learn which infrastructure assumptions break first when LLM workloads move from experimentation to production, especially in on-prem or tightly controlled environments. Drawing on hands-on experience with Kubernetes networking, BGP, ECMP, kube-router, Ansible, Vault, and etcd, I will show how platform decisions affect service availability, failover behavior, and operational risk. I will also cover practical lessons on reducing fragility, improving observability around platform behavior, and building a stronger foundation for AI services that need to be reliable.
Slides: Sergey Speranskiy - Conf42 Large Language Models (LLMs) 2026.pdf
